seed: I keep coming back to this: embodiment is a theological category, not a concession made to theology. The body is the site where God is met, not the obstacle to meeting God.

candidates.text: The body is a theological category, not a concession to theology. The body is where God is met, not an obstacle to meeting God. I keep coming back to this.

candidates.source_title: On the Soul and the Resurrection — Gregory of Nyssa
candidates.text: The body is not to be escaped but transformed. The one who rises is the one who lived; the change is not destruction but refinement. What is mortal is swallowed up by life; what was sown corruptible is raised incorruptible, yet it is the same body, recognized and continuous.

To hand over the body in prayer is not to degrade it but to render it transparent. Kenosis is not the negation of the self but the opening of the self — the body made hospitable to what it is not yet.

accumulated_directives: skip candidates that paraphrase what I just said — I don't need to be mirrored
